description: This API routine executes the third release of the ANSI fault calculation function. Use this API routine to calculate fault currents according to the ANSI standard (activity ANSI). To designate fault specification data as part of the API’s calling sequence, INARG must be positive; to designate fault specification data in an ANSI Fault Specification Data input file, INARG must be zero and the filename specified as FILARG. This API routine was first introduced in release 34.2.0. ierr = ansi_3(options, values, inarg, busary, nodary, vltary, cptary, filarg)
python_syntax: ierr = ansi_3(options, values, inarg, busary, nodary, vltary, cptary, filarg)

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: An array of five elements specifying solution and reporting options. (input).

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: An array of four elements specifying divisors for calculating resistances of network elements where resistance is zero in the working case. (input).

parameters.name: busary
parameters.description: An array of INARG elements specifying a list of faulted buses; ignored if INARG is 0 (if INARG > 0). (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: nodary
parameters.description: An array of INARG elements specifying a list of node numbers whose bus sections are to be faulted; ignored if INARG is 0 (if INARG > 0). (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: vltary
parameters.description: An array of INARG elements specifying a list of maximum operating voltages in pu; ignored if INARG is 0. (input; 1.0 by default).

parameters.name: cptary
parameters.description: An array of INARG elements specifying a list of contact parting times in seconds; ignored if INARG is 0. (input; 1 cycle by default).

parameters.name: filarg
parameters.description: Filename of ansi fault specification data file; ignored if inarg > 0 (if inarg is 0). (input; no default allowed).

options.description: fault analysis generator reactance option (0 by default).
use subtransient reactance.
use transient reactance.
use synchronous reactance.
transformer impedance correction option (1 by default).
do not apply to zero sequence.
apply to zero sequence.
set of ANSI decrement factors to use (1 by default).
account for dc decrement only (use ANSI table 3).
account for ac and dc decrements (use ANSI tables 1 and 2).
option (0 by default).
summary output using ANSI X only, report format (blank delimited output fields).
summary output using ANSI X only, spreadsheet input format (comma delimited output fields).
detailed output using ANSI X only.
detailed output using ANSI R and X.
summary output using ANSI R and X, report format (blank delimited output fields).
summary output using ANSI R and X, spreadsheet input format (comma delimited output fields).
breaker rating option (0 by default).
IEEE total current basis.
IEEE symmetrical current basis.

values.description: used for branches in the positive sequence network (40.0 by default).
used for machines in the positive sequence network (80.0 by default).
used for branches in the zero sequence network (40.0 by default).
used for machines in the zero sequence network (80.0 by default).

inarg.description: fault specifications specified in input file.
fault specifications specified in calling sequence.

error_codes.description: fault analysis is invalid due to the presence of in-service induction machines that are in the “stalled” or “tripped” state.
bus type code and series element status inconsistencies.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
